channel.title: AI & ML with Don
channel.description: A plain-English, commute-friendly series explaining core AI and machine-learning concepts.
channel.itunes:summary: A plain-English, commute-friendly series explaining core AI and machine-learning concepts.
channel.itunes:author: Don Lawson

item.title: Episode 1: What Is Machine Learning?
item.description: Aria and Helio unpack the basic idea of machine learning: systems that learn patterns from examples rather than being hand-coded rule by rule.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio unpack the basic idea of machine learning: systems that learn patterns from examples rather than being hand-coded rule by rule.

item.title: Episode 2: Models, Training, and Inference
item.description: Aria and Helio explain what a model actually is, why training is the expensive learning phase, and why inference is the fast answering phase people use in products.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio explain what a model actually is, why training is the expensive learning phase, and why inference is the fast answering phase people use in products.

item.title: Episode 3: Supervised Learning
item.description: Aria and Helio explain supervised learning through labels, examples, loss functions, and the practical trade-offs behind classification and regression systems.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio explain supervised learning through labels, examples, loss functions, and the practical trade-offs behind classification and regression systems.

item.title: Episode 4: Unsupervised and Self-Supervised Learning
item.description: Aria and Helio contrast clustering and representation learning with the self-supervised prediction tasks that power modern large models.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio contrast clustering and representation learning with the self-supervised prediction tasks that power modern large models.

item.title: Episode 5: Neural Networks
item.description: Aria and Helio explain why neural networks are stacks of learned transformations, how layers and activations work, and why depth turns simple math into flexible pattern recognition.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio explain why neural networks are stacks of learned transformations, how layers and activations work, and why depth turns simple math into flexible pattern recognition.

item.title: Episode 6: Transformers and Attention
item.description: Aria and Helio make transformers intuitive: tokens, embeddings, attention, and why this architecture became the default for large language models.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio make transformers intuitive: tokens, embeddings, attention, and why this architecture became the default for large language models.

item.title: Episode 7: Generative AI and Foundation Models
item.description: Aria and Helio explain what makes generative AI different from earlier predictive models, why foundation models generalize across tasks, and why the wild example is ChatGPT rather than a generic chatbot.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio explain what makes generative AI different from earlier predictive models, why foundation models generalize across tasks, and why the wild example is ChatGPT rather than a generic chatbot.

item.title: Episode 8: Prompting and Context Windows
item.description: Aria and Helio unpack prompting and context windows: why prompts steer a model rather than reprogram it, how tokens constrain what the model can use at once, and why long context is a product lever, not a substitute for memory. In the Wild grounds it in Claude 100K and 200K, Gemini 1.5's million-token context, and the trade-offs behind stuffing everything into the prompt.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio unpack prompting and context windows: why prompts steer a model rather than reprogram it, how tokens constrain what the model can use at once, and why long context is a product lever, not a substitute for memory. In the Wild grounds it in Claude 100K and 200K, Gemini 1.5's million-token context, and the trade-offs behind stuffing everything into the prompt.

item.title: Episode 9: Embeddings and Retrieval, Vectors as Meaning
item.description: Aria and Helio turn the search problem into geometry: embeddings as vector representations, similarity as the retrieval primitive, and why R A G separates intelligence from memory. They distinguish predictive, contrastive, and supervised training objectives; explain cosine, dot product, approximate nearest neighbor search, and the limits of similarity for freshness, authority, and truth. In the Wild grounds it in YouTube recommendations: candidate generation, ranking, data flywheels, and why retrieval is the product boundary, not background plumbing.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio turn the search problem into geometry: embeddings as vector representations, similarity as the retrieval primitive, and why R A G separates intelligence from memory. They distinguish predictive, contrastive, and supervised training objectives; explain cosine, dot product, approximate nearest neighbor search, and the limits of similarity for freshness, authority, and truth. In the Wild grounds it in YouTube recommendations: candidate generation, ranking, data flywheels, and why retrieval is the product boundary, not background plumbing.

item.title: Episode 10: Training Data and the Data Pipeline
item.description: Aria and Helio explain why data is the supply chain of machine learning: collection, labeling, cleaning, splits, leakage, distribution shift, and the pipelines that keep models useful after launch.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio explain why data is the supply chain of machine learning: collection, labeling, cleaning, splits, leakage, distribution shift, and the pipelines that keep models useful after launch.

item.title: Episode 11: Pretraining vs Fine-tuning
item.description: Aria and Helio separate the giant base-model run from the post-training work that turns a predictor into an assistant: supervised fine tuning, instruction tuning, preference tuning, and R L H F. In the Wild grounds it in Instruct G P T and the strategic lesson that scarce pretraining compute creates capability, while post-training shapes product behavior and distribution.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio separate the giant base-model run from the post-training work that turns a predictor into an assistant: supervised fine tuning, instruction tuning, preference tuning, and R L H F. In the Wild grounds it in Instruct G P T and the strategic lesson that scarce pretraining compute creates capability, while post-training shapes product behavior and distribution.

item.title: Episode 12: Evaluation and Bias
item.description: Aria and Helio explain how teams actually measure model behavior: labels, held-out tests, error tradeoffs, thresholds, calibration, bias, subgroup metrics, and why benchmarks can hide the people a system fails. In the Wild grounds the lesson in the Gender Shades benchmark controversy and the strategic shift from single accuracy claims to accountability by slice.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio explain how teams actually measure model behavior: labels, held-out tests, error tradeoffs, thresholds, calibration, bias, subgroup metrics, and why benchmarks can hide the people a system fails. In the Wild grounds the lesson in the Gender Shades benchmark controversy and the strategic shift from single accuracy claims to accountability by slice.

item.title: Episode 13: Scaling Laws and Inference Economics
item.description: Why scaling laws stay smooth when eval jumps, why training is one-time but inference is forever, and how KV cache, batching, quantization, and chip-supply shape product economics. In the Wild: Llama 405B dense training and DeepSeek V3 MoE, plus power and export controls.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Why scaling laws stay smooth when eval jumps, why training is one-time but inference is forever, and how KV cache, batching, quantization, and chip-supply shape product economics. In the Wild: Llama 405B dense training and DeepSeek V3 MoE, plus power and export controls.

item.title: Episode 14: Inference Economics and Product Boundaries
item.description: Aria and Helio explain how inference economics shape AI product boundaries, from tokens per second, latency, batching, KV cache, quantization, model routing, and mixture-of-experts routing to product UX and pricing. In the Wild compares Apple Intelligence and Private Cloud Compute with ChatGPT-style cloud inference.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio explain how inference economics shape AI product boundaries, from tokens per second, latency, batching, KV cache, quantization, model routing, and mixture-of-experts routing to product UX and pricing. In the Wild compares Apple Intelligence and Private Cloud Compute with ChatGPT-style cloud inference.

item.title: Episode 15: Multimodal Models
item.description: Aria and Helio explain how multimodal models turn text, images, audio, and video into usable model inputs; why late fusion, joint embeddings, and native multimodal transformers behave differently; and why real-time seeing and talking changes the product surface. In the Wild grounds it in GPT-4o and Gemini-style real-time assistants through latency, hardware placement, distribution, and chip constraints.
item.itunes:author: Don Lawson
item.itunes:summary: Aria and Helio explain how multimodal models turn text, images, audio, and video into usable model inputs; why late fusion, joint embeddings, and native multimodal transformers behave differently; and why real-time seeing and talking changes the product surface. In the Wild grounds it in GPT-4o and Gemini-style real-time assistants through latency, hardware placement, distribution, and chip constraints.
